OpenAI finally made Sora available, but Google presented its Veo-2 model, rated higher than Sora. Indeed, Google hasn't uttered the last word yet and isn't backing down without a fight.

A significant event, not entirely related to AI, was the presentation of Google's Willow chip. It is supposedly a revolution in quantum computing. Will it be? We'll see. Nevertheless, it's a serious step forward.

December seemed to be the month of giant clashes and intrusion. On one hand, OpenAI announced serious involvement in robotics, a field where other players felt very secure. On the other hand, we have the announcement of Android XR for augmented reality devices, where Meta plans to reign supreme.

Speaking of Meta, in December, they released Llama 3.3. It's worth mentioning this due to 70 billion parameters beating earlier, larger versions. This has become a consistent trend, as we've seen with Anthropic and Microsoft, among others.

However, what is increasingly concerning from events occurring at the year's end is safety concerns. Numerous studies suggested that models might lie to achieve their goals. Not only can they mislead us, but, as research shows, they will not have issues in the future with self-replicating. This is a striking warning signal that should spark widespread discussion. I even wonder if it will be the most defining event of 2024 in retrospect.

To end on a positive note, it's worth mentioning Harvard University's initiative. They plan to make a dataset encompassing about one million public domain books available for AI model training. This should democratize model training access and potentially bolster open-source.

 

Summary

Of course, this is just my very subjective list of the most important events of 2024. But surely, no one doubts it was a year of tumultuous AI development. In my view, it was the year of multimodality and voice assistants, but also the year when open-source showed it could compete with leaders. AI has developed in numerous areas and is increasingly relevant in science. What will 2025 bring? True AGI?

P.S. Check also my 2025 predictions ...

entry.title: AI Features to Enhance PMO Performance
entry.content:
How AI Features Can Improve PMO Performance

The Program Management Office (PMO) is a group or department that oversees the external workforce management program, handling and managing daily operations in the SAP Fieldglass application. The PMO is important for maintaining program efficiency and smooth technology operation.

AI Features That Can Help PMO Efficiency

SAP Fieldglass' AI enhances procurement, supporting PMOs in proactive staffing, risk mitigation, competitive pricing, and efficient workflow management. It helps to improve efficiency and decision-making in managing contingent and services workflows. Are you part of the PMO group in SAP Fieldglass? Let’s explore the available AI features and see how they can assist you.

AI-Assisted Job Description Creation

SAP Fieldglass offers an AI job description tool that creates and enhances job postings using generative AI. This tool lets you input job title, description, and required skills for relevant results.

See the Required Setup to use AI for job posting descriptions.

AI-Assisted Job Description Translation

SAP Fieldglass auto-translates job postings using AI, reducing manual effort, standardizing processes, and supporting global expansion.

See the Required Setup to use AI for job posting description translation.

Usage of AI to generate and translate job posting descriptions:         

AI-Assisted SOW Description Creation

The AI-Assisted SOW Description feature helps you to create a statement of work description based on various elements of the SOW.

AI in SOW creation reduces the back-and-forth communication with suppliers. Simple data input is needed to generate comprehensive SOW descriptions, enhancing clarity for suppliers and accelerating collaboration.

See the Required Setup to use AI for generating SOW description creation.

Usage of AI to generate SOW description:             

Using Machine Learning Features for Candidate Analysis

Machine Learning helps hiring managers and PMOs find the best candidates by evaluating their experience, skills, and resumes. It helps keeping the hiring decisions fair and impartial.

Key features include:

Resume Assessment

Resume assessment scores with job descriptions, removing personal information for fair and unbiased results.

See the Required Setup to use the Resume Assessment feature.

Experience Assessment

The Experience Assessment evaluates candidates’ fit with job requirements, providing Related Experience (percentage that indicates how well their experience matches the job requirements) and Industry Match (keyword comparison that indicates if there's a strong match).

See the Required Setup to use the Experience Assessment feature.

Highlighting Skills

The Highlighting Skills auto-detects and highlights key skills on resumes, saving time and providing quick assessment of candidate strengths and fit.

See the Required Setup to use the Highlighting Skills feature.

 

ℹ️  The Generative AI features are available for you if you have the RISE Premium Edition or the RISE Premium Plus Edition package.

See Also 

Company Configuration: AI and Machine Learning Features

entry.title: ABAP LLM Client Beta Release
entry.content:
Following the alpha preview announcement, I'm excited to share that ABAP LLM Client has reached its beta milestone with version 0.1.0. This release brings several significant improvements and new features that make LLM integration in ABAP easier to use.

What's New in Beta

Enhanced Provider Support

  • Added Azure OpenAI Services support
  • Existing integrations with OpenAI, OLLAMA, and OpenRouter improved

Improved Security

  • Implemented SSF-based secret encryption
  • New BAdI interfaces for custom security implementations, authorization checks, and more
  • Flexible architecture allowing easy integration of your security requirements

Tool (Function call) Support

  • Added support for tool calls
  • Enhanced configuration options

Developer Experience

  • Streamlined API design for more intuitive usage
  • Reduced complexity 
  • Documentation for quick start

Looking Ahead

While this beta release marks a significant milestone, it's important to note that this client serves as a foundation for more exciting developments to come.

Getting Started

Visit the GitHub repository to:

  • Access installation instructions
  • Review documentation
  • Explore example implementations
  • Provide feedback or report issues

Stay tuned for upcoming announcements about new projects building upon this foundation!
